businessSummary.oneLiner: Southeast Asia's largest e-commerce (Shopee), digital entertainment (Garena/Free Fire), and fintech (Monee) operator — Singapore-based global internet company
businessSummary.description: Sea Limited is a Singapore-based global tech company operating e-commerce (Shopee), gaming (Garena), and digital financial services (Monee) across Southeast Asia, Latin America, and Taiwan. TTM revenue $21.04B (+35.85% YoY). Q4 2025 earnings releasing March 3 pre-market. EPS $0.81-0.90 expected, revenue +38% growth anticipated. Down 47% from 52-week high of $199.30. P/E 45x, Forward P/E 23.92x, PEG 0.55.
howTheyMakeMoney: E-commerce (Shopee): #1 SE Asia e-commerce platform. GMV-based commissions and advertising revenue
Digital Entertainment (Garena): Free Fire and other game operations. In revival cycle
Digital Financial Services (Monee/SeaMoney): Digital payments, lending, insurance. Fintech growth
Other: Logistics (Shopee Express), food delivery, etc.
keyProducts: Shopee: #1 e-commerce platform in SE Asia and Latin America
Free Fire: Global hit battle royale mobile game
SeaMoney/Monee: Digital payments and financial services

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Early-stage earnings turnaround. Converging rapidly to Forward P/E 23.92x

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS $4.40. PEG 0.55 signals undervaluation vs growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth
keyMetrics.interpretation: Driven by Shopee GMV growth and fintech expansion

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: E-commerce + gaming blended margin. Gradually improving

keyMetrics.name: Debt/Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Moderate levels. LT Debt/Eq 0.13 — long-term debt healthy

keyMetrics.name: PEG
keyMetrics.interpretation: Clear undervaluation signal vs 43.37% 5-year EPS growth rate

growthDrivers.title: Q4 2025 Earnings D-1 — EPS $0.81-0.90 Expected
growthDrivers.description: March 3 pre-market release. EPS expected to grow 45-50% YoY, revenue +38%. Potential to beat Zacks consensus.

growthDrivers.title: Shopee SE Asia and Latin America GMV Acceleration
growthDrivers.description: Expanding e-commerce penetration in SE Asia and Brazil market growth driving high GMV growth.

growthDrivers.title: Fintech (Monee) Lending and Insurance Expansion
growthDrivers.description: SeaMoney digital financial services growing rapidly in synergy with Shopee ecosystem.

growthDrivers.title: Free Fire Revival Cycle — User Recovery
growthDrivers.description: Garena's Free Fire recovering user base since India relaunch. Gaming segment bottom confirmed.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: SE Asia e-commerce leader (Shopee), benefiting from 670M population digitalization. E-commerce-fintech-gaming ecosystem synergy is the core moat.

moats.description: Shopee's two-sided seller-buyer network. More sellers → more buyers.
#1 e-commerce in 6 SE Asian countries. Self-built logistics infrastructure (Shopee Express).

competitors.name: Lazada (Alibaba)
competitors.detail: #2 SE Asia e-commerce. Well-funded by Alibaba but market share gap widening

competitors.name: TikTok Shop
competitors.detail: Social commerce surging in SE Asia. Shopee's biggest threat

competitors.name: MercadoLibre (MELI)
competitors.detail: #1 Latin America e-commerce. Direct competition with Shopee in Brazil

competitors.name: Grab (GRAB)
competitors.detail: SE Asia super-app. Competing in fintech and delivery

recentNews.significance: March 3 pre-market release. 45-50% EPS growth, 38% revenue growth anticipated.

recentNews.significance: EPS $0.81 consensus. Revenue also expected to surge significantly YoY.

recentNews.significance: Shopee GMV, SeaMoney credit portfolio, Garena bookings and other key metrics previewed.

recentNews.significance: Underperforming broader market. Earnings surprise could trigger rebound.

recentNews.headline: SE -5.42% Weekly — $105.21 Near 52-Week Low
recentNews.significance: Just 5.7% above 52-week low of $99.50. Pre-earnings anxiety reflected.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 11 of 12 analysts Buy. Strong Buy consensus $189.41 (+80%). Current $105 is 47% below 52-week high — earnings surprise could trigger massive rebound.

risks.title: Down 47% From 52-Week High — Downtrend Persists
risks.description: Sharp decline from $199.30 to $105. Technically in strong downtrend, near 52-week low of $99.50.

risks.title: TikTok Shop Competitive Threat
risks.description: TikTok Shop's rapid social commerce growth in SE Asia could erode Shopee's GMV growth.

risks.title: SE Asia Macro Uncertainty
risks.description: Strong dollar and local currency weakness negatively impact revenue translation. Consumer spending slowdown risk.

risks.title: Gaming Segment Uncertainty
risks.description: Free Fire revival sustainability unclear. Gaming revenue contribution may continue declining.

risks.title: Latin America Profitability
risks.description: Shopee Brazil growing but lagging MercadoLibre in profitability. Subsidy competition ongoing.

buyReasons.title: PEG 0.55 + Forward P/E 23.92x — Historically Undervalued
buyReasons.rationale: 43% 5-year EPS growth rate vs Forward P/E 24x is undervalued. PEG 0.55 is a clear buy signal.

buyReasons.title: Earnings Catalyst D-1 — Surprise Could Trigger Major Rebound
buyReasons.rationale: Q4 earnings on March 3. EPS 45-50% growth expected. Earnings beat near 52-week low could spark 20%+ rally.

buyReasons.title: 11 of 12 Analysts Buy — Target $189 (+80%)
buyReasons.rationale: Overwhelming buy-side support. 80% upside to consensus. Strong Buy consensus.

buyReasons.title: SE Asia E-Commerce Structural Growth — Penetration Expansion
buyReasons.rationale: 670M population with expanding e-commerce penetration. Shopee's #1 position provides structural tailwind.

overallOpinion: Mar 2 close $105.21 (-5.42% weekly). Near 52-week low of $99.50. Q4 2025 earnings on March 3 pre-market — EPS $0.81-0.90, revenue +38% growth expected. Earnings is the biggest catalyst.
11 of 12 analysts Buy. Strong Buy consensus $189.41 (+80%). Forward P/E 23.92x, PEG 0.55 — extremely undervalued relative to growth.
$105.21, below both 50-day ($120) and 200-day ($143) MAs. RSI 39 approaching oversold. Earnings surprise could trigger technical rebound.
